comment: Specialized purine nucleoside phosphorylase acting on 5′-fluoro-5′-deoxyadenosine; essential for generating fluorinated intermediates in organofluorine biosynthesis (FAc and 4-FT)

changelog.comment: New entry. Reviewer 1 adjusted nomenclature. Reviewer 2 fixed MIBiG cross-links.

reactions.description: Phosphate-dependent purine nucleoside phosphorylase that catalyzes the reversible phosphorolysis of 5′-fluoro-5′-deoxyadenosine (5′-FDA), yielding adenine and a fluorinated sugar phosphate. This reaction constitutes a key step in microbial organofluorine biosynthesis, channeling fluorinated intermediates toward fluoroacetate and 4-fluorothreonine production. Gene inactivation abolishes downstream fluorometabolite formation, supporting its essential role in pathway flux.
Phosphate-dependent phosphorolysis of 5′-fluoro-5′-deoxyadenosine, cleaving the N-glycosidic bond to yield adenine and a fluorinated ribose-1-phosphate (5-fluoro-5-deoxyribose-1-phosphate)

evidenceCode: Inference from genomic data and chemical structure
